pages.text: Novus runs $(italic)Simple Voice Chat$() — real, $(bold)proximity-based$() voice. Speak and the players near you hear you; walk away and your voice fades with the distance. No outside program, no account; it travels with the server.$(br2)It turns a shared world into a place you're actually $(italic)in$() together — calling across a mine, narrating a build, hearing someone before you see them.
pages.title: Voice Chat

pages.text: $(bold)Setting it up.$() Open the $(italic)Voice Chat$() menu from your controls (and bind a $(italic)push-to-talk$() key, or let it open on your voice). The first time, point it at the right microphone and set your volume — there's an in-game settings screen for all of it.$(br2)$(li)Voice is $(bold)opt-in$(): if you say nothing and bind nothing, you're silent.$(li)You can $(bold)mute$() anyone, or yourself, at any time.$(li)It needs to be on the $(italic)server$() to work — and Novus' server has it.
pages.title: Setting It Up
